# Harness Builder Skill

Use this skill when the task is to design, implement, or explain an AI agent harness.

Required structure:

1. Define the runtime boundary: task, context, planner/model, tools, loop, final answer.
2. Split memory into procedural, semantic, and episodic stores.
3. Put every tool behind permission checks.
4. Add loop guardrails: max iterations, stop condition, blocked state, and approval boundary.
5. Trace every run as an event tree.
6. Evaluate the trajectory, not only the final answer.
7. Gate prompt/config/tool/RAG changes before release.

Design rule:

Let the model think probabilistically; make the harness act deterministically.
LLM Ops is the outer improvement loop: trace runs, evaluate quality, observe health, diagnose root causes, and gate releases.

## Reconstructed Tutorial: Agent Harness + LLM Ops

The image is teaching a simple but important architecture:

> **The LLM is not the product. The harness is the product.**
> The model is a stochastic policy inside a larger deterministic runtime that controls context, memory, tools, permissions, tracing, evaluation, and release.

The diagram has two big systems:

| Layer       | Purpose                                                                                                           |
| ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Harness** | Runs the agent: prompt assembly, memory retrieval, tool loop, working context, guardrails, reply generation.      |
| **LLM Ops** | Measures and improves the harness: traces, evals, observability, diagnosis, release gates, config/prompt updates. |

The left side is the **agent runtime**.
The right side is the **production improvement loop**.

---

# 1. What “Harness” Means

A **harness** is the engineered control shell around the LLM.

It includes:

```text
system prompt
user prompt
chat history
working memory
long-term memory
tool registry
permissions
retrieval
agent loop
guardrails
state machine
traces
evals
release gates
```

So the model is only one component:

```text
Agent = LLM + context assembler + memory + tools + loop + guardrails + evaluator + observability
```

The image labels this red box as:

```text
Harness
e.g. LangGraph, LangChain, Pydantic
```

That is accurate

# Harness Builder Skill

Use this skill when the task is to design, implement, or explain an AI agent harness.

Required structure:

1. Define the runtime boundary: task, context, planner/model, tools, loop, final answer.
2. Split memory into procedural, semantic, and episodic stores.
3. Put every tool behind permission checks.
4. Add loop guardrails: max iterations, stop condition, blocked state, and approval boundary.
5. Trace every run as an event tree.
6. Evaluate the trajectory, not only the final answer.
7. Gate prompt/config/tool/RAG changes before release.

Design rule:

Let the model think probabilistically; make the harness act deterministically.
LLM Ops is the outer improvement loop: trace runs, evaluate quality, observe health, diagnose root causes, and gate releases.

## Reconstructed Tutorial: Agent Harness + LLM Ops

The image is teaching a simple but important architecture:

> **The LLM is not the product. The harness is the product.**
> The model is a stochastic policy inside a larger deterministic runtime that controls context, memory, tools, permissions, tracing, evaluation, and release.

The diagram has two big systems:

| Layer       | Purpose                                                                                                           |
| ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Harness** | Runs the agent: prompt assembly, memory retrieval, tool loop, working context, guardrails, reply generation.      |
| **LLM Ops** | Measures and improves the harness: traces, evals, observability, diagnosis, release gates, config/prompt updates. |

The left side is the **agent runtime**.
The right side is the **production improvement loop**.

---

# 1. What “Harness” Means

A **harness** is the engineered control shell around the LLM.

It includes:

```text
system prompt
user prompt
chat history
working memory
long-term memory
tool registry
permissions
retrieval
agent loop
guardrails
state machine
traces
evals
release gates
```

So the model is only one component:

```text
Agent = LLM + context assembler + memory + tools + loop + guardrails + evaluator + observability
```

The image labels this red box as:

```text
Harness
e.g. LangGraph, LangChain, Pydantic
```

That is accurate

The image is teaching a simple but important architecture:

> **The LLM is not the product. The harness is the product.**
> The model is a stochastic policy inside a larger deterministic runtime that controls context, memory, tools, permissions, tracing, evaluation, and release.

The diagram has two big systems:

| Layer       | Purpose                                                                                                           |
| ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Harness** | Runs the agent: prompt assembly, memory retrieval, tool loop, working context, guardrails, reply generation.      |
| **LLM Ops** | Measures and improves the harness: traces, evals, observability, diagnosis, release gates, config/prompt updates. |

The left side is the **agent runtime**.
The right side is the **production improvement loop**.

---

# 1. What “Harness” Means

A **harness** is the engineered control shell around the LLM.

It includes:

```text
system prompt
user prompt
chat history
working memory
long-term memory
tool registry
permissions
retrieval
agent loop
guardrails
state machine
traces
evals
release gates
```

So the model is only one component:

```text
Agent = LLM + context assembler + memory + tools + loop + guardrails + evaluator + observability
```

The image labels this red box as:

```text
Harness
e.g. LangGraph, LangChain, Pydantic
```

That is accurate
